blurb: Named for Boulder itself — a thornless, arching native shrub hung with big white rose-like flowers in late spring over handsome maple-shaped leaves; tough, shade-tolerant, and a foothills classic.
water: Low to moderate; xeric once established
seasons: Large white flowers late spring–early summer; maple-like leaves turning yellow in fall; dry reddish fruit
wildlife: Native bees; fruit and cover for birds
